It belongs to the genus Brachiaria, which comprises several species of grasses commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Brachiaria is further classified under the subfamily Chloridoideae and the order Poales. intro: Brachiaria glabrinodis, commonly known as Borreria glabrinodis, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
